title: Beads Orchestra: Workflow Orchestration for AI Agent Coordination
description:
Transform beads from a passive issue tracker into an active AI agent coordinator.

## Value Proposition

Automatic work dispatch when dependencies resolve, built-in retry/timeout handling, intelligent load balancing across agent capabilities, and full execution observability.

## Architecture

- Uses existing custom tables pattern (documented in EXTENDING.md)
- Runs within existing daemon infrastructure
- Three new tables: orchestration_executions, orchestration_agents, orchestration_workflows
- Four core components: Engine, AgentRegistry, ExecutionManager, Dispatcher

## Implementation Plan

See ORCHESTRATION_PLAN.md for complete 70-page implementation plan.

## Phases

0. Foundation (Week 1) - Schema and package setup
1. Core Engine (Week 2-3) - Orchestration loop implementation
2. CLI Integration (Week 3-4) - User-facing commands
3. Dispatcher (Week 4-5) - Protocol implementations
4. Observability (Week 5-6) - Polish and production-ready features

## Success Metrics

- 5+ teams using in beta
- 99%+ execution success rate
- <100ms dispatch latency (p95)
- NPS > 50

title: Create internal/orchestration package structure
description:
Set up the foundation for orchestration functionality.

## Tasks

1. Create directory structure:
   ```
   internal/orchestration/
   ├── schema.go           # Table definitions
   ├── schema_test.go      # Schema tests
   ├── types.go            # Execution, Agent, Workflow types
   └── README.md           # Package overview
   ```

2. Define core types in types.go:
   - Execution struct
   - Agent struct
   - Status enum
   - ExecutionFilter struct

3. Write package documentation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Package compiles without errors
- [ ] README.md documents package purpose
- [ ] All types have godoc comments
- [ ] Unit tests pass

title: Define orchestration database schema
description:
Design and document the complete database schema for orchestration.

## Tables to Define

1. **orchestration_executions** - execution tracking
2. **orchestration_agents** - agent registry
3. **orchestration_workflows** - workflow definitions (future)

## Requirements

- Use SQLite-compatible syntax
- Include foreign key constraints
- Add appropriate indexes for performance
- Document each column with comments
- Define views for common queries

## Files

- internal/orchestration/schema.go
- Complete SQL DDL statements as constants

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Schema compiles and validates
- [ ] Foreign keys reference correct tables
- [ ] Indexes cover common query patterns
- [ ] Schema documented in code comments

title: Implement database initialization logic
description:
Create InitializeSchema function that creates orchestration tables.

## Implementation

Use existing custom tables pattern via UnderlyingDB()

## Files

- internal/orchestration/schema.go (InitializeSchema function)
- internal/orchestration/schema_test.go (initialization tests)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] InitializeSchema creates all tables
- [ ] Idempotent (safe to call multiple times)
- [ ] Returns error on failure
- [ ] Tables created with correct constraints
- [ ] Unit tests verify table creation

title: Write comprehensive schema tests
description:
Test orchestration schema creation and constraints.

## Test Cases

1. Table creation verification
2. Foreign key constraints
3. Index validation
4. Check constraints
5. Idempotency

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 100% code coverage for schema.go
- [ ] All constraints tested
- [ ] Foreign keys validated
- [ ] Tests use in-memory SQLite database

title: Create orchestration documentation
description:
Write comprehensive documentation for the orchestration package.

## Documents

1. internal/orchestration/README.md
2. docs/orchestration.md (user guide)
3. Update ORCHESTRATION_PLAN.md as needed

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README.md covers all components
- [ ] User guide has working examples
- [ ] All public functions have godoc comments
